decisionPanels.title: 🚨 LucidLink Directory Management API + Guest Access — direct MMR accelerators
decisionPanels.summary: LucidLink's January 2026 release delivered two features with immediate MMR relevance. First: Guest Access — external collaborators can now review and download assets directly in-browser, no LucidLink account or install required. Authentication is via a temporary email code; zero-knowledge security maintained. This solves the external review problem for multimedia producers without third-party transfer tools. Second: Directory Management API — programmatic folder structure and permissions management at scale. This means Ricky's Project Launcher can extend beyond iconik collection creation to also automate LucidLink folder hierarchies and access controls — a single trigger, two platforms, fully set up. Both features are available now on new LucidLink plans.
actions: Evaluate LucidLink Guest Access for multimedia producer external review workflows — eliminates current 'upload it somewhere' workarounds.

Prototype Project Launcher extension using the Directory Management API to auto-create LucidLink folder structures and permissions when a new production is started — same trigger as iconik collection creation.
Document the API integration in Project Launcher roadmap before MMR go-live, even if not shipping in v1.

decisionPanels.title: ⚠️ iconik My Shares filtering (live March 5) + ongoing design system rollout
decisionPanels.summary: iconik's March 5 changelog added My Shares filtering: producers can now filter shares by All, Shared with Me, or Shared by Me. Alongside this, favourite saved searches are now bookmarkable. The design system rollout (grey background, new nav, no Upload button) that went live March 4 continues — dark mode and full accessibility work are planned throughout 2026. These are live in production for Guardian producers today. The design system will continue to evolve so training materials need to be considered living documents, not one-off guides.
actions: Update MMR producer documentation and onboarding materials to reflect the new iconik UI (grey background, admin dropdown, no Upload button, My Shares filters).
Communicate the My Shares filtering feature to multimedia producers — particularly useful for producers receiving shared review links.
Plan for at least one more iconik UI update round before year-end as dark mode and accessibility work lands — build review checkpoints into MMR maintenance calendar.

decisionPanels.title: 📊 SPUR coalition + agentic AI: Guardian is both protecting content AND about to automate with it
decisionPanels.summary: On February 26, The Guardian joined BBC, FT, Sky News, and Telegraph Media Group as founding members of SPUR (Standards for Publisher Usage Rights) — an industry coalition establishing technical standards for how AI can use journalistic content. Simultaneously, Reuters Institute predicts that 2026 is the year agentic AI moves from pilot to real-world newsroom workflow automation: end-to-end investigations, fact-checking, and newsgathering. A provocative data point: more bots than people may soon be reading publisher websites. The Guardian's dual position (protecting content + using AI internally) is becoming clearer as institutional strategy — not contradiction.
actions: Monitor SPUR coalition progress for any technical metadata/tagging standards that might affect how multimedia content is tagged or licensed in iconik.
Keep MMR metadata schema flexible: SPUR may require new rights/licensing fields for video and audio assets — future-proof iconik custom metadata now.

Watch for Guardian internal AI policy updates following SPUR launch — agentic AI for newsroom workflows will likely accelerate, which means MMR becomes foundational infrastructure sooner than expected.

decisionPanels.title: 📱 Vertical +120%, YouTube Shorts as discovery layer — Guardian Studios' format roadmap
decisionPanels.summary: Backlight's 2026 Media Stats Report shows vertical publishing up 120% year-on-year across its platform, though landscape (16:9) still dominates at 90%+. YouTube CEO Neal Mohan's 2026 letter makes Shorts the primary discovery mechanism, with long-form as the depth/monetisation layer. Creators are now running like full studios — episodic formats, not one-offs. Guardian Studios (headed by Leah Green) exists precisely to build this model. NAB 2026 (April 18-22) will feature Harmonic's AI orchestration, Backlight's single-platform create/manage/publish strategy, and AI asset protection — all directly relevant to Guardian's video-first build.
actions: Ensure MMR output format configurations support vertical (9:16) as a first-class asset type alongside 16:9 — especially for Project Launcher metadata templates.
Use YouTube Shorts = discovery, long-form = depth as a framing conversation with Guardian Studios when they engage with MMR workflows.
Track NAB 2026 (April 18-22) announcements from Backlight, Harmonic, and Avid for iconik/LucidLink product reveals that may affect MMR roadmap.

items.title: iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering (All/Shared with Me/Shared by Me) + favourite saved searches

LucidLink January 2026 release: Guest Access (browser-based external review, zero-knowledge security, email auth, no install required)
LucidLink Directory Management API: programmatic folder structure, permissions, access controls at scale — directly relevant to Project Launcher automation

NAB Show 2026 (April 18-22): Harmonic unveiling next-gen media server + AI orchestration; Backlight presenting single-platform manage-create-publish
Guardian Studios formed Feb 26 2026: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — video-first, personality-led journalism mandate, new demand on MMR
Guardian joins SPUR coalition (Feb 26): BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph founding members — technical standards for AI use of publisher content
Guardian debuts flagship video podcast Feb 2026: US co-hosted show, staff of 10, competing with NYT and NPR
YouTube CEO 2026 strategy: Shorts = discovery layer, long-form = depth/monetisation, creators = studios — integrated format system required
Wildmoka: TikTok Drafts manual publish outperforms automated schedulers by up to 60% reach
Meta Graph API v25.0: Page Viewer metric replaces legacy reach by mid-2026 — analytics dashboards must update
Reuters Institute 2026: agentic AI for end-to-end newsroom workflow automation becomes real this year — investigations, fact-checking, newsgathering
items.source: Reuters Institute for the Study of Journalism

items.title: Reuters Institute warning: more bots than people may soon be reading publisher websites — fundamentally changes digital distribution assumptions
Avid CPO at NAB 2026: AI deployed to 'remove friction in the workflow' without replacing creative control or editorial intent

newItems.title: iconik changelog March 5: My Shares filtering + favourite saved searches now live
LucidLink Guest Access: browser-based external review, no account required, zero-knowledge security
LucidLink Directory Management API: automate folder structures + permissions at scale
SPUR coalition launched: Guardian + BBC + FT + Sky + Telegraph — technical AI content licensing standards
Reuters Institute: agentic AI for newsroom workflow automation is real in 2026 — investigations, fact-checking, end-to-end automation

changes.summary: Tonight's standout signal: LucidLink's Jan 2026 release (Guest Access + Directory Management API) is directly actionable for MMR — external review without accounts, and Project Launcher can now extend to auto-create LucidLink folder structures. iconik My Shares filtering is live in production. The SPUR coalition crystallises The Guardian's AI strategy: protect content externally, automate internally. Agentic AI for end-to-end newsroom workflows is no longer prediction — it's 2026 reality.
